name: District of Columbia v. Wesby
question:
1. Do officers have probable cause to arrest for unlawful entry under D.C. law despite a claim of good-faith entry?
  2. Was the law sufficiently clearly established to justify the denial of immunity to the officers?
description: A case in which the Court decided that (1) officers had probable cause to arrest a number of partygoers for unlawful entry under D.C. law under the totality of the circumstances, and (2) that the officers were entitled to qualified immunity because their actions were not clearly unlawful at the time.
